channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: Childcare safety reforms enter new phase as Minister warns more services could lose funding
item.description: Australia's early childhood education and care sector has entered a new phase of regulatory enforcement, with Minister for Education Jason Clare signalling that services which fail to meet child safety standards should expect to lose Commonwealth funding.
item.content:encoded: Australia's early childhood education and care sector has entered a new phase of regulatory enforcement, with Minister for Education Jason Clare signalling that services which fail to meet child safety standards should expect to lose Commonwealth funding.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: EOFY review: A landmark year for child safety reform in Australia's ECEC sector
item.description: The end of the financial year is traditionally a time for reflection across Australia's early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector. It provides an opportunity for providers, educators and policy makers to review the year that was, assess progress and prepare for the challenges and opportunities ahead.
item.content:encoded: The end of the financial year is traditionally a time for reflection across Australia's early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector. It provides an opportunity for providers, educators and policy makers to review the year that was, assess progress and prepare for the challenges and opportunities ahead.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: OOSH Educators Day 2026 shines a spotlight on the professionals behind school-age care
item.description: Every day, outside school hours (OOSH) educators create safe, engaging and inclusive environments where children can play, learn and build meaningful relationships. OOSH Educators Day 2026 is an opportunity to recognise the professionalism, dedication and lasting impact of the workforce supporting children before school, after school and during vacation care.
item.content:encoded: Every day, outside school hours (OOSH) educators create safe, engaging and inclusive environments where children can play, learn and build meaningful relationships. OOSH Educators Day 2026 is an opportunity to recognise the professionalism, dedication and lasting impact of the workforce supporting children before school, after school and during vacation care.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Why energetic play is essential for holistic child development
item.description: Research shows that nine in 10 young children are not meeting the recommended levels of daily physical activity, despite strong evidence that movement is fundamental to children's learning, health and wellbeing. For early childhood education and care (ECEC) services, creating opportunities for energetic play is not simply good practice, it is an important part of delivering high-quality education and care.
item.content:encoded: Research shows that nine in 10 young children are not meeting the recommended levels of daily physical activity, despite strong evidence that movement is fundamental to children's learning, health and wellbeing. For early childhood education and care (ECEC) services, creating opportunities for energetic play is not simply good practice, it is an important part of delivering high-quality education and care.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Disability Standards for education changes: what ECEC providers need to know before 1 August
item.description: Major amendments to the Disability Standards for Education 2005 (the Standards) will take effect on 1 August 2026, extending the Standards to most early childhood education and care (ECEC) services for the first time.
item.content:encoded: Major amendments to the Disability Standards for Education 2005 (the Standards) will take effect on 1 August 2026, extending the Standards to most early childhood education and care (ECEC) services for the first time.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Childcare service cops historic penalty over rat poison, blames govt ‘witch hunt’ - Herald Sun
item.description: Childcare service cops historic penalty over rat poison, blames govt ‘witch hunt’ - Herald Sun
item.content:encoded: Childcare service cops historic penalty over rat poison, blames govt ‘witch hunt’ - Herald Sun
item.dc:creator: Herald Sun

item.title: Renewal of the wage subsidy great news for educators, children, families and gender equity
item.description: The renewal of the Australian Government’s 15% early learning wage subsidy marks a significant step forward for the sector, recognising the essential role educators play in supporting children’s learning and development.
item.content:encoded: The renewal of the Australian Government’s 15% early learning wage subsidy marks a significant step forward for the sector, recognising the essential role educators play in supporting children’s learning and development.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: ACCS child wellbeing language update aims to reduce stigma and support better family conversations
item.description: The Australian Government has updated the language used to describe children who may be eligible for Additional Child Care Subsidy (ACCS) child wellbeing, with the changes taking effect from 1 July 2026.
item.content:encoded: The Australian Government has updated the language used to describe children who may be eligible for Additional Child Care Subsidy (ACCS) child wellbeing, with the changes taking effect from 1 July 2026.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.content:encoded: Melbourne childcare service hit with historic penalty blames government ‘witch hunt’ - Herald Sun

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 26 June 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, national attention turns to educator workforce investment, continued coverage of child protection reforms in Queensland and the NT, and Children’s Day.

item.title: Shaping Bright Futures: WorkinEarlyLearning launches to tackle the ECEC Sector's workforce crisis - The Sector
item.description: Shaping Bright Futures: WorkinEarlyLearning launches to tackle the ECEC Sector's workforce crisis - The Sector

item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Thriving Kids must be built with Aboriginal communities to succeed
item.description: SNAICC - National Voice for our Children has released a new policy paper calling on governments to design and deliver the Thriving Kids initiative in genuine partnership with Aboriginal Community-Controlled Organisations (ACCOs).
item.content:encoded: SNAICC - National Voice for our Children has released a new policy paper calling on governments to design and deliver the Thriving Kids initiative in genuine partnership with Aboriginal Community-Controlled Organisations (ACCOs).
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Everyone experiences loss and grief – but that doesn’t make it any easier to talk with kids about it
item.description: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.content:encoded: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Marlee Silva – National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children’s Day 2026 Ambassador
item.description: Marlee Silva is the National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children’s Day 2026 Ambassador! Find out more in the Ambassador Video!
item.content:encoded: Marlee Silva is the National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children’s Day 2026 Ambassador! Find out more in the Ambassador Video!
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: The Breadwinner: Comedy doesn’t need flawless dads, but we do need competent ones
item.description: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.content:encoded: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.description: SNAICC responds to Worker Retention Payment extension, NT and Queensland child protection scrutiny, Aboriginal Family-Led Decision Making and Children’s Day.

item.title: Extension of early childhood worker retention payment welcome amid ongoing reform efforts - thepoint.com.au
item.description: Extension of early childhood worker retention payment welcome amid ongoing reform efforts - thepoint.com.au
item.content:encoded: Extension of early childhood worker retention payment welcome amid ongoing reform efforts - thepoint.com.au
item.dc:creator: thepoint.com.au

item.title: Childcare workers have been guaranteed a pay bump. What’s the fine print?
item.description: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.content:encoded: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Investing in educators means investing in children
item.description: SNAICC commends the Federal Government backing hardworking early education and care (ECEC) workers by extending the Worker Retention Payment. Catherine Liddle, CEO of SNAICC said the extension of the 15% wage increase is a welcome and necessary investment in the ECEC workforce.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC commends the Federal Government backing hardworking early education and care (ECEC) workers by extending the Worker Retention Payment. Catherine Liddle, CEO of SNAICC said the extension of the 15% wage increase is a welcome and necessary investment in the ECEC workforce.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Toy Story 5 pits traditional toys vs a tablet. In real life, families can combine the two
item.description: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
item.content:encoded: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?

item.title: Report shows how young people want to use their voice for change
item.description: Young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people want authentic representation, decision-making power and community-led structures that deliver real outcomes, according to a landmark national report released today.

item.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples ready to work in partnership for a safer future for Queensland children
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children CEO Catherine Liddle has urged the Queensland Government to work in genuine partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and communities in reforming the State’s child protection system.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children CEO Catherine Liddle has urged the Queensland Government to work in genuine partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and communities in reforming the State’s child protection system.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 5 June 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to proposed NT child protection laws and the Children’s Commissioner’s report, and announces Marlee Silva as 2026 Children’s Day Ambassador.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to proposed NT child protection laws and the Children’s Commissioner’s report, and announces Marlee Silva as 2026 Children’s Day Ambassador.
